title: SOUL Atlas Metadata
description: Structured metadata describing a single SOUL. Lives beside SOUL.md as metadata.yaml.

title.description: Human-readable name of the way of thinking, e.g. 'Software Engineer'.

slug.description: URL-safe identifier; must match the directory name.

aliases.description: Alternative names and common synonyms used in search.

kind.description: What kind of mind this SOUL portrays. The Atlas began with occupations and is branching out: a 'discipline' or 'role' need not be a job, an 'identity' or 'community' is a way of thinking that never came with a title, and 'agent-persona' covers AI personality templates federated from external sources. Absent means 'occupation' for backward compatibility.

tags.description: Free-form descriptive tags for faceted browsing.

difficulty.description: Rough depth of expertise required to perform the role well.

summary.description: One- or two-sentence essence of how this mind works. Shown in cards and search.

contributors.description: GitHub handles or names of people who shaped this SOUL.

provenance.description: How this SOUL was first drafted. Honesty about origin is a trust signal: 'ai-generated' means a model wrote the first draft and no practitioner has verified it yet.

source.description: Where this SOUL came from, when it did not originate in this repository. Used to attribute and link back to third-party collections (e.g. souls.directory) that the Atlas federates rather than authors. Absent means the SOUL originated here.

origin.description: Short identifier of the upstream collection, e.g. 'souls.directory'.

url.description: Canonical URL of the entry in the upstream collection.

license.description: License the upstream entry is distributed under, e.g. 'MIT'.

attribution.description: Human-readable credit for the original author(s).

fetched.description: ISO date this SOUL was last fetched from the upstream source.

last_reviewed.description: ISO date a human reviewed this SOUL for accuracy. Null means never reviewed by a person.

reviewers.description: People who have reviewed this SOUL against real-world practice. Ideally practitioners who do (or have done) the work.

credential.description: How they know the field, e.g. '15 years as a climbing arborist'.

created.description: ISO date this SOUL was created.

updated.description: ISO date this SOUL was last substantively updated.

related.description: Typed relationships to other SOULs, used to build the knowledge graph.

specializations.description: Narrower variants of this role (also expressed as related[type=specialization]).

country_variants.description: Notable ways the role differs across countries/jurisdictions.

sources.description: References, standards, or bodies of knowledge this SOUL draws on.
